page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m1.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. The server features 4 dedicated physical cores utilizing AMD EPYC processors running at a clock speed of 2.25 GHz. It is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance type, and it does not include any graphics processing units.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps and does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. A key architectural tradeoff of this configuration is its highly constrained memory-to-CPU ratio of 1:1. With only 4 GB of RAM allocated across 4 dedicated vCPUs, memory-intensive applications will face severe resource bottlenecks. Additionally, the lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment for any persistent data needs.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation and low memory footprint, the ecs.u2a-c1m1.xlarge is qualitatively positioned as a budget-friendly option for compute-centric workloads that require dedicated processing power but have minimal memory requirements. Suitable workloads include lightweight CPU-bound tasks, basic batch processing, microservices, and simple utility servers that do not demand large memory buffers or local disk caching.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m1.xlarge is an x86_64 virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores running at 2.25 GHz. The instance is configured with 4.0 GB of RAM, establishing a low 1:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core. It contains no local storage and no GPU accelerators, relying entirely on external network storage. Network connectivity is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile presents a clear tradeoff, offering dedicated compute resources at the expense of memory capacity. Consequently, the instance is best suited for lightweight, CPU-bound workloads, batch processing, and microservices with minimal memory footprints.

tagline: A dedicated compute instance featuring a highly streamlined memory profile and baseline network performance for lightweight CPU-bound workloads.
